context: Northstar contract: /api/v1/oauth3/scopes is the SYSTEM CATALOG of available OAuth3 scope names — intentionally public. Agents must be able to enumerate available scopes before requesting them in OAuth3 flows. This is analogous to a public OpenID Connect discovery endpoint. What requires auth is: user-specific granted scopes, token introspection. James Bach: distinguish between 'what scopes exist' (public catalog) vs 'what scopes this user has' (PII, must be auth-gated). Endpoint correctly returns 200 with scope list without auth.
